rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical intramolecular oxidative dearomative spirocyclization of indole-tethered naphthoquinone amines to give a spiroindoline-fused naphthoquinone via radical intermediates
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of the indole/aminonaphthoquinone substrate generates a nitrogen- or carbon-centered radical (and/or radical cation) that undergoes intramolecular C–C bond formation onto the indole C3, with subsequent oxidation/deprotonation to yield the spirocyclic product

key_intermediates.name: substrate-derived nitrogen/carbon radical (indolyl or aminyl radical) generated by anodic SET
indole C3 radical adduct / spirocyclic radical intermediate trapped by BHT to give compound 32
BHT-trapped open-chain adduct 33 (phenol coupled to indole C2/C3 position of substrate)

electron_flow: The anode oxidizes the indole-tethered aminonaphthoquinone substrate by single-electron transfer to give a delocalized N/C-centered radical; intramolecular radical addition onto the indole forms the spiro C–C bond, and further oxidation/deprotonation (with cathodic H2 evolution) furnishes the spiroindolenine 2.

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Product 2 obtained only in trace amount, indicating a radical intermediate is intercepted

probe_reagents.name: BHT
probe_reagents.observation: Product 2 only in trace; two BHT-derived radical adducts 32 (C35H37N2O3+, [M+H]+ found 533.2858) and 33 ([M+Na]+ found 557.2817) detected by HRMS, consistent with trapping of substrate-derived radicals at the indole position

probe_reagents.name: 1,1-Diphenylethylene
probe_reagents.observation: Product 2 still formed in 26% yield; DPE only partially inhibits the reaction, supporting but not strongly suppressing the radical pathway

notes: Mechanistic support is based solely on radical-trapping experiments (TEMPO, BHT, 1,1-diphenylethylene) with HRMS detection of BHT adducts 32 and 33; no DFT, CV, or KIE studies are shown on these pages. The BHT adducts indicate that both an indole-centered carbon radical (32) and possibly an aminyl/aminonaphthoquinone-centered radical (33) are accessible intermediates, suggesting a stepwise radical cyclization pathway rather than a concerted process.
